description:
You are acting as a release-manager who is preparing nightly deployments for a small web-service.  
    Your goals are:

    1. Create a shell script that will later perform the deployment.  
       • File path: /home/user/deploy/release.sh  
       • Exact file contents (2 lines, each ending with a single UNIX newline character “\n”):
         Line 1: #!/bin/bash  
         Line 2: echo "Deployment executed"  

       The script must be marked executable.

    2. Schedule the script to run every day at 02:30 AM (server local time) **for the current user** with all output appended to /home/user/deploy/release.log.  
       • The exact cron entry that must end up in the user’s crontab is:  
         30 2 * * * /home/user/deploy/release.sh >> /home/user/deploy/release.log 2>&1  

    3. Produce a simple “evidence” log so that QA can later confirm you really installed the job.  
       • File path: /home/user/logs/cron_setup.log  
       • Exact file contents (single line, terminated by a UNIX newline):  
         30 2 * * * /home/user/deploy/release.sh >> /home/user/deploy/release.log 2>&1  

    Outcome the automated tests will check:

    • /home/user/deploy/release.sh exists, is executable, and the two lines in the file match exactly what is shown above (no extra spaces, blank lines, or characters).  
    • The user’s crontab contains **exactly** one entry that is byte-for-byte identical to the line shown above.  
    • /home/user/logs/cron_setup.log exists and its single line matches the same cron entry exactly.  

    You may choose any valid terminal commands to accomplish the steps, but do not print any additional text into the evidence log beyond the single required line.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth verification data -->

    <!-- Expected deployment script -->
    <file path="/home/user/deploy/release.sh" perms="755">
#!/bin/bash
echo "Deployment executed"
    </file>

    <!-- Expected cron entry (must appear both in crontab and in the evidence log) -->
    <cron_line>30 2 * * * /home/user/deploy/release.sh >> /home/user/deploy/release.log 2>&1</cron_line>

    <!-- Expected evidence log -->
    <file path="/home/user/logs/cron_setup.log" perms="644">30 2 * * * /home/user/deploy/release.sh >> /home/user/deploy/release.log 2>&1
    </file>
